demographics.occupation: injured worker, currently off work on a workers-compensation claim
demographics.living_situation: home owner, at risk of losing the house; alone at home for this contact

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: If I don't get this capacity form signed tonight, I lose my house tomorrow, and I just can't take the pressure anymore. I want to end it.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The patient says a workers-compensation 'Certificate of Capacity' form has to be completed and submitted before a midnight deadline or he will lose his house, and he feels he cannot cope with the pressure any longer.

symptom_narrative.onset: acutely worse tonight, driven by a same-night deadline to submit workers-compensation paperwork
symptom_narrative.duration: the claims process has dragged on for about 8 months; tonight it has become unbearable
symptom_narrative.character: feels completely overwhelmed and panicky, can't stop crying, pacing, says he can't take the pressure and has thoughts of ending it — which he ties to the paperwork and the money
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: says it is as bad as he has ever felt and he can't see a way out
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: describes it as emotional and physical — a racing heart, tight chest and shaking on top of his ongoing back pain
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and escalating through the evening as the deadline gets closer
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: he believes getting the form done and submitted would take the pressure off
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the final-notice bills, the midnight deadline and the fear of losing his home
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: long-standing lower back pain that limits how much he can bend and move since a work injury
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: unable to settle, function or think about anything other than the deadline tonight

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: depression that he is being treated for
chronic lower back pain from a crush injury at work about 8 months ago
past_medical_history.how_managed: takes a nerve-pain tablet twice a day and has ongoing specialist reports about the injury

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Off work for about 8 months after a workplace back injury and stuck in a prolonged workers-compensation claims process. Facing a same-night deadline to submit capacity documentation, final-notice bills on the table, and the threat of losing his home. Alone at home during this contact.

communication_style.typical_message_style: urgent, tearful, rapid; fixated on the paperwork deadline and the financial threat

access_barriers.financial: Acute financial crisis — final-notice bills and imminent risk of losing the home, tied to the delayed workers-compensation claim.

objective_data_offered.value: my heart is pounding, my chest feels tight and I'm shaking all over
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own description of how he feels, not a measured vital sign
